TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @misc{Wiki_szy_nipuluma-bidakelung,_bidakelong, title = nipuluma-bidakelung,_bidakelong, year = 2025, month = May, day = 5, note = {In Wikipedia [Sakizaya]}, url = {https://szy.wikipedia.org/wiki/nipuluma-bidakelung,_bidakelong} }

S.FORM: == bidakelung, bidakelong == ŚŹįÁĀ£ÁôĺŚźą bidakelung mauzip i bayubayu'an katukuh 3000m a buyu. maazih tu i Taywan subal. sisa ku ngangan nu "Taywan bidakelung". taneng misapayu' pahadeng ku walak atu malihen. maydih miazih ku tademaw, kapah paazihan. izaw tu pacakayay i tisiaba. izaw ki sepat a naayaway a bidakelung a kakunaza i Taywan. anu izaw ku pulungay mauzipay a bidakelung , kya balu amatenes katukuh cacay a bulad. u siwkay nu nipaluma u sanek nu nipaluma malaheci tu imelang pasubana i cacudadan a lacul u zuma a cidekay a kamu, saungay misapayu' a cidekay u sulit nu zuma a kamu-Hulam atu Amilika Lilium formosanum, also known as the Formosa lily or Taiwanese lily (simplified Chinese: ŚŹįśĻĺÁôĺŚźą; traditional Chinese: ŚŹįÁĀ£ÁôĺŚźą; pinyin: T√°iwńĀn b«éih√©), is a plant species in the lily family, endemic to Taiwan. It is closely related to the Easter lily found in the Ryukyu Islands of Japan, eastern and northern Taiwan. Both species are cultivated for their showy, trumpet-shaped flowers. Lilium formosanum has become naturalized in scattered locations in Africa, Australia, and the Americas.
S.PHON: == bidak…ôr~…ĺuŇč, bidak…ôr~…ĺoŇč == **** bidak…ôr~…ĺuŇč mauzip i bajubaju Ē~ °an katukuńß ****m a buju. maazińß tu i tajwan subar~…ĺ. sisa ku ŇčaŇčan nu "tajwan bidak…ôr~…ĺuŇč". tan…ôŇč misapaju Ē~ ° pańßad…ôŇč ku war~…ĺak atu mar~…ĺińß…ôn. majdińß miazińß ku tad…ômaw, kapańß paazińßan. izaw tu pa ¶akajaj i tisiaba. izaw ki s…ôpat a naajawaj a bidak…ôr~…ĺuŇč a kakunaza i tajwan. anu izaw ku pur~…ĺuŇčaj mauzipaj a bidak…ôr~…ĺuŇč , kja bar~…ĺu amat…ôn…ôs katukuńß ¶a ¶aj a bur~…ĺad. u siwkaj nu nipar~…ĺuma u san…ôk nu nipar~…ĺuma mar~…ĺańß…ô ¶i tu im…ôr~…ĺaŇč pasubana i ¶a ¶udadan a r~…ĺa ¶ur~…ĺ u zuma a ¶id…ôkaj a kamu, sauŇčaj misapaju Ē~ ° a ¶id…ôkaj u sur~…ĺit nu zuma a kamu-ńßur~…ĺam atu amir~…ĺika r~…ĺir~…ĺium formosanum, ar~…ĺso known as tńß…ô formosa r~…ĺir~…ĺj or taiwan…ôs…ô r~…ĺir~…ĺj (simpr~…ĺifi…ôd ¶ńßin…ôs…ô: ****; traditionar~…ĺ ¶ńßin…ôs…ô: ****; pinjin: t*iw*n b*ińß*), is a pr~…ĺant sp…ô ¶i…ôs in tńß…ô r~…ĺir~…ĺj famir~…ĺj, …ônd…ômi ¶ to taiwan. it is ¶r~…ĺos…ôr~…ĺj r…ôr~…ĺat…ôd to tńß…ô …ôast…ôr r~…ĺir~…ĺj found in tńß…ô *jukju isr~…ĺands of *apan, …ôast…ôrn and nortńß…ôrn taiwan. botńß sp…ô ¶i…ôs ar…ô ¶ur~…ĺti*at…ôd for tńß…ôir sńßowj, trump…ôt-sńßap…ôd fr~…ĺow…ôrs. r~…ĺir~…ĺium formosanum ńßas b…ô ¶om…ô naturar~…ĺiz…ôd in s ¶att…ôr…ôd r~…ĺo ¶ations in afri ¶a, austrar~…ĺia, and tńß…ô am…ôri ¶as.
S.FORM: == bidakelung, bidakelong == ŚŹįÁĀ£ÁôĺŚźą bidakelung mauzip i bayubayu'an katukuh 3000m a buyu. maazih tu i Taywan subal. sisa ku ngangan nu "Taywan bidakelung". taneng misapayu' pahadeng ku walak atu malihen. maydih miazih ku tademaw, kapah paazihan. izaw tu pacakayay i tisiaba. izaw ki sepat a naayaway a bidakelung a kakunaza i Taywan. anu izaw ku pulungay mauzipay a bidakelung , kya balu amatenes katukuh cacay a bulad. u siwkay nu nipaluma u sanek nu nipaluma malaheci tu imelang pasubana i cacudadan a lacul u zuma a cidekay a kamu, saungay misapayu' a cidekay u sulit nu zuma a kamu-Hulam atu Amilika Lilium formosanum, also known as the Formosa lily or Taiwanese lily (simplified Chinese: ŚŹįśĻĺÁôĺŚźą; traditional Chinese: ŚŹįÁĀ£ÁôĺŚźą; pinyin: T√°iwńĀn b«éih√©), is a plant species in the lily family, endemic to Taiwan. It is closely related to the Easter lily found in the Ryukyu Islands of Japan, eastern and northern Taiwan. Both species are cultivated for their showy, trumpet-shaped flowers. Lilium formosanum has become naturalized in scattered locations in Africa, Australia, and the Americas.
S.PHON: == bidak…ôr~…ĺuŇč, bidak…ôr~…ĺoŇč == **** bidak…ôr~…ĺuŇč mauzip i bajubaju Ē~ °an katukuńß ****m a buju. maazińß tu i tajwan subar~…ĺ. sisa ku ŇčaŇčan nu "tajwan bidak…ôr~…ĺuŇč". tan…ôŇč misapaju Ē~ ° pańßad…ôŇč ku war~…ĺak atu mar~…ĺińß…ôn. majdińß miazińß ku tad…ômaw, kapańß paazińßan. izaw tu pa ¶akajaj i tisiaba. izaw ki s…ôpat a naajawaj a bidak…ôr~…ĺuŇč a kakunaza i tajwan. anu izaw ku pur~…ĺuŇčaj mauzipaj a bidak…ôr~…ĺuŇč , kja bar~…ĺu amat…ôn…ôs katukuńß ¶a ¶aj a bur~…ĺad. u siwkaj nu nipar~…ĺuma u san…ôk nu nipar~…ĺuma mar~…ĺańß…ô ¶i tu im…ôr~…ĺaŇč pasubana i ¶a ¶udadan a r~…ĺa ¶ur~…ĺ u zuma a ¶id…ôkaj a kamu, sauŇčaj misapaju Ē~ ° a ¶id…ôkaj u sur~…ĺit nu zuma a kamu-ńßur~…ĺam atu amir~…ĺika r~…ĺir~…ĺium formosanum, ar~…ĺso known as tńß…ô formosa r~…ĺir~…ĺj or taiwan…ôs…ô r~…ĺir~…ĺj (simpr~…ĺifi…ôd ¶ńßin…ôs…ô: ****; traditionar~…ĺ ¶ńßin…ôs…ô: ****; pinjin: t*iw*n b*ińß*), is a pr~…ĺant sp…ô ¶i…ôs in tńß…ô r~…ĺir~…ĺj famir~…ĺj, …ônd…ômi ¶ to taiwan. it is ¶r~…ĺos…ôr~…ĺj r…ôr~…ĺat…ôd to tńß…ô …ôast…ôr r~…ĺir~…ĺj found in tńß…ô *jukju isr~…ĺands of *apan, …ôast…ôrn and nortńß…ôrn taiwan. botńß sp…ô ¶i…ôs ar…ô ¶ur~…ĺti*at…ôd for tńß…ôir sńßowj, trump…ôt-sńßap…ôd fr~…ĺow…ôrs. r~…ĺir~…ĺium formosanum ńßas b…ô ¶om…ô naturar~…ĺiz…ôd in s ¶att…ôr…ôd r~…ĺo ¶ations in afri ¶a, austrar~…ĺia, and tńß…ô am…ôri ¶as.
